word_list.word: birds in their little nests agree

senses.definition: used to imply that people are more likely to get along well with each other when they are in comfortable and familiar surroundings

examples.x: The sports team played better on their home field than when they were away, showing that birds in their little nests agree also applies to team dynamics in competitive settings.

senses.definition: used to say that people who are similar or share a common goal should not harm each other, emphasizing the importance of loyalty and unity within a group
used to promote the idea that people should prioritize love and peaceful relationships over war and violence

word_list.word: a lean agreement is (far|way|much) better than a fat lawsuit

senses.definition: used to imply that it is better to find mutually acceptable solutions to conflicts, rather than resorting to legal action that can be time-consuming, expensive, and emotionally draining

word_list.word: it takes two to make a bargain (rolling|)
word_list.pronunciation: …™t tňąe…™ks tňąuňź t…ô mňĆe…™k …ź bňą…Ďňź…Ļ…°…™n …Ļňąo äl…™Ňč …Ēňź…Ļ

senses.definition: used to imply that both parties involved in a negotiation or agreement must be willing to cooperate and compromise in order for it to be successful

examples.x: The mediator reminded the disputing parties that it takes two to make a bargain, and encouraged them to listen to each other's needs and work together to find a solution that satisfied both parties.

word_list.word: it takes two to make a quarrel

senses.definition: used to imply that both parties are responsible for a disagreement or argument, and neither can be solely blamed for it

examples.x: John and Jane were both yelling at each other during the argument, but later realized that it takes two to make a quarrel, and they both needed to work on their communication skills.

word_list.word: the quarrel of lovers is the renewal of love
word_list.pronunciation: √į…ô kwňą…Ēňź…Ļ…ôl  Ćv lňą Ćv…öz …™z √į…ô …Ļ…™nňąuňź…ôl  Ćv lňą Ćv

senses.definition: used to imply that conflicts or arguments between two people in a romantic relationship are a natural and even necessary part of the relationship's growth and renewal

examples.x: When Jake and Maria had a disagreement, they remembered that the quarrel of lovers is the renewal of love, and they worked together to find a solution that strengthened their relationship.

word_list.word: truth is the first casualty of war
word_list.pronunciation: t…Ļňąuňźőł …™z √į…ô fňą…úňźst kňą√¶ íuňź…ôl…ĺi  Ćv wňą…Ēňź…Ļ

senses.definition: used to imply that during times of conflict, the truth is often distorted or hidden because conflicting sides manipulate information to gain an advantage

examples.x: During the investigation into the harassment allegations, both the accused employee and the victim were found to have distorted the truth, showcasing how truth is the first casualty of war.
